zh.lifeSummary: Lady Isabel Saville於1474年出生在英國英格蘭越可爾德斯費爾德。她於1498年與Edward Marshe在英國英格蘭越可爾德斯費爾德結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1534年享年60歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Lady Isabel Saville于1474年出生在英国英格兰越可爾德斯費爾德。她于1498年与Edward Marshe在英国英格兰越可爾德斯費爾德结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于1534年享年60岁。

nameMeanings.name: Saville
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin; Yorkshire): habitational name from a place in northern France of which the identity is not clear, though it is probably Sainville in Eure-et-Loire, so called from Old French saisne ‘Saxon’ + ville ‘settlement’. Compare Savel , Savell , and Savelle .

variants.name: Savel
meanings: Slovak and Czech (Šavel): from the Biblical personal name Šavel, Hebrew Shaul (see Saul ). It may be a nickname for a godless person, from the archaic word šavel, which is derived from the Biblical name.

Slovenian (Šavel): probably from an old spelling of the Biblical personal name Savel (see Saul , compare 2 above). Alternatively, perhaps a nickname or a topographic name from an old vernacular spelling of the same plant name as in 4 below.
Americanized form of Slovenian Šavelj: probably a nickname or a topographic name from the plant name š(č)avelj ‘sorrel (Rumex acetosa)’.

variants.name: Savelle
meanings: English: variant of   Saville  . This form of the surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Saffell
meanings: English (Essex): from the Middle English personal name Safugel (Old Norse sǽfogl ‘sea-bird, especially the cormorant’), not recorded as a personal name in Scandinavia, but common in England after the Norman Conquest. In some cases with voicing of intervocalic /f/ to /v/, there may have been confusion with Saville .

nameMeanings.name: Lady
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English lavedi, levedi, ladi ‘lady, female head of a household, woman, wife’ (Old English hlǣfdige). As a man's name it may have been used in mockery, or be short for occupational names such as Ladyman.
Hungarian (Ládi): habitational name for someone from Lád in Borsod County or Lad in Somogy County.

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

en.lifeSummary: Lady Isabel Saville was born in 1474, in Huddersfield, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. She married Edward Marshe in 1498, in Huddersfield, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died in 1534, at the age of 60.

fr.lifeSummary: Lady Isabel Saville est née en 1474, à Huddersfield, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Elle a épousé Edward Marshe en 1498, à Huddersfield, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée en 1534, à l’âge de 60 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Lady Isabel Saville wurde 1474 in Huddersfield, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom geboren. Sie hat 1498 in Huddersfield, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom Edward Marshe geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb 1534 im Alter von 60 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Lady Isabel Saville è nata il nel 1474 nel Huddersfield, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lei sposato Edward Marshe nel 1498, nel Huddersfield, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1534, all'età di 60 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Isabel Savilleは1474年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアハッドエルスフィールドで生まれた。1498年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアハッドエルスフィールドでEdward Marsheと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は1534年に60歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Isabel Saville(은)는 1474년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 허더스필드에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1498년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 허더스필드에서 Edward Marshe(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1534년 60세의 나이로 사망했다.
